what: Styling Shiny Apps with CRUK Branding
title: Styling Shiny Apps with CRUK Branding
text: shinyCRUK provides Cancer Research UK-branded components building Shiny applications align CRUK’s Helix Design System brand guidelines.

title: Styling Shiny Apps with CRUK Branding
text: Pre-styled components following CRUK brand guidelines Consistent typography using Progress Medium Poppins fonts CRUK color palette visual identity Responsive layouts mobile desktop

title: Styling Shiny Apps with CRUK Branding
text: Navigation: Branded nav bar responsive logos selectors Layout: Central column containers optimal content width Fonts: Page titles headings CRUK styling Value Boxes: Styled value boxes Footers: Complete footer legal information contact details Utilities: Rounding functions per CI guidelines

title: Styling Shiny Apps with CRUK Branding
text: Comprehensive function documentation available package website. function includes: Detailed parameter descriptions Usage examples Accessibility guidelines

title: Styling Shiny Apps with CRUK Branding
text: Install development version GitHub:
code: # Using pak pak::pak("CRUKorg/shinyCRUK")  # Or using remotes remotes::install_github("CRUKorg/shinyCRUK")

title: Styling Shiny Apps with CRUK Branding
text: ’s minimal example CRUK-branded Shiny app:
code: library(shiny) library(shinyCRUK) library(bslib) library(shinyWidgets)  options(shiny.autoreload = TRUE)   # Define UI for application that draws a histogram ui <- bslib::page_navbar(   tags$head(crukGA(), crukTheme()),   # Navbar title   fillable = FALSE,   title = crukNavTitle("{shinyCRUK}", selectors = 1,                        selector1 = radioGroupButtons(inputId = "word-select",                                                      label = "Selector label",                                                      choices = c("A", "B", "C"),                                                      width = "100%",                                                      justified = TRUE)),   # header = "",   footer = centralColumn(crukFooter()),    nav_panel(title = "Overview",             centralColumn(               lastReview(format(as.Date(Sys.Date()), "%d %B %Y"), tag = "hp"),               crukTitle("Welcome to the {shinyCRUK} test app",                         "This app will dsiplay some of the functions that are built into the app"),               p("This package is mostly intended to make it easier to make pretty shiny apps. But it also comes with our rounding rules.",                 "So for example, you can use crukRounding() so numbers like 12236 become ", crukRounding(12236),                 " or you can use it for percentages so 0.652 becomes ", crukRoundingPercentage(0.652, "lower"), "."),               layout_column_wrap(                 width = 1/2,                 crukValueBox(id = "valuBox1",                              title = "Title of the first value box",                              value = crukRounding("785849615"),                              icon = icon("weight-scale"),                              detail = "detail text",                 ),                 crukValueBox(id = "valuBox2",                              title = "Title of the second value box",                              value = crukRoundingPercentage(0.456186),                              icon = icon("weight-scale"),                              detail = "detail text",                 )               )              )   ),    nav_panel(title = "Logos",             centralColumn(               lastReviewInternal(format(as.Date(Sys.Date()), "%d %B %Y")),               crukTitle("The CRUK logo",                         "This app will dsiplay some of the functions that are built into the app"),               p("I've built up a couple of functions to make it easier to get the CRUK logo."),               p("You can call crukLogo() which will give you the logo at a standard 50 pixels tall.", br(),                 crukLogo()),               p("But if you want you can change the size, so for example crukLogo('125px')", br(),                 crukLogo("125px")),               br(),               p("You might notice this app uses the wide logo, which can be called with crukLogoWide(), but we are only meant to use this when absolutely necessary...", br(),                 crukLogoWide(75))             )) )  # Define server logic required to draw a histogram server <- function(input, output) {  }  # Run the application shinyApp(ui = ui, server = server)

title: Styling Shiny Apps with CRUK Branding
code: # Apply CRUK theme tags$head(crukTheme())  # Create central content column (900px max width) centralColumn(   # Your content here )

title: Styling Shiny Apps with CRUK Branding
text: Navbar styling handles desktop mobile, two selectors navbar.
code: # Simple navigation crukNavTitle(Title = "Early Diagnosis Data Hub")  # With selectors crukNavTitle(   Title = "Data Dashboard",   selectors = 2,   selector1 = selectInput("year", "Year:", 2020:2024),   selector2 = selectInput("region", "Region:", c("UK", "England", "Scotland", "Wales", "Northern Ireland")) )

title: Styling Shiny Apps with CRUK Branding
code: # Page title with optional subheading crukTitle(   "Cancer Incidence Trends",   "Age-standardised rates per 100,000 population" )

title: Styling Shiny Apps with CRUK Branding
code: # Stacked logo (preferred) crukLogo(height = "50px")  # Wide logo (use sparingly) crukLogoWide(height = "35px")

title: Styling Shiny Apps with CRUK Branding
code: crukValueBox(   id = "metric1",   title = "Total Cases",   value = crukRounding(1234),   icon = shiny::icon("chart-bar"),   icon_colour = "#00007e",  # CRUK blue   detail = "Year ending 2024" )

title: Styling Shiny Apps with CRUK Branding
text: Use top pages users know date material .
code: # Public content lastReview("20 October 2025")  # Health professional content lastReview("20 October 2025", tag = "health_professional")  # Internal content lastReview("20 October 2025", tag = "internal")  # Accepts multiple date formats lastReview("20/10/2025")              # UK format lastReview(as.Date("2025-10-20"))     # Date object lastReview(Sys.Date())                # Current date

title: Styling Shiny Apps with CRUK Branding
code: # Full footer with contact information crukFooter()  # Footer without contact section  crukFooter(includeContact = FALSE)

title: Styling Shiny Apps with CRUK Branding
code: # Format numbers per CRUK Intelligence guidelines crukRounding(1200) # Returns: "around 1,200"  crukRounding(156789) # Returns: "around 157,000"

title: Central Column Container — centralColumn
text: Creates responsive central column container main page content. div provides centered, fixed-width content area responsive whitespace either side, following CRUK design patterns optimal readability visual hierarchy.

title: Central Column Container — centralColumn
code: centralColumn(...)

title: Central Column Container — centralColumn
text: ... UI elements placed within central column. Can include Shiny HTML elements text, plots, tables, components. arguments automatically wrapped central column container.

title: Central Column Container — centralColumn
text: htmltools::tagList containing: htmlDependency loads centralColumn.css div element class "central-column" wrapping       provided content

title: Central Column Container — centralColumn
text: container wrap main page elements except navigation bar, creating consistent content width across different screen sizes maintaining appropriate margins.

title: Central Column Container — centralColumn
text: central column adapts different screen sizes: Desktop Fixed width 900px, centered automatic side margins Tablet/Mobile Full width responsive padding maintain     readability smaller screens exact responsive breakpoints styling defined inst/www/css/centralColumn.css.

title: Central Column Container — centralColumn
text: CSS class: "central-column" Stylesheet: centralColumn.css Maximum content width: 900px (desktop)

title: Central Column Container — centralColumn
text: Typical page structure using function:
code: ui <- fluidPage(   tags$head(crukTheme()),   crukNavTitle(Title = "My App"),  # Outside centralColumn   centralColumn(                    # Everything else inside     # All main content here   ) )

title: Central Column Container — centralColumn
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Basic usage with a title centralColumn(   crukTitle("Page Title") )  # Multiple elements centralColumn(   crukTitle("Dashboard"),   p("Welcome to the data dashboard."),   plotOutput("mainPlot") )  # With various content types centralColumn(   crukTitle("Analysis Results"),   h3("Summary Statistics"),   tableOutput("summaryTable"),   h3("Visualization"),   plotOutput("chart"),   textOutput("notes") )  # Complete Shiny app structure library(shiny) library(shinyCRUK)  ui <- fluidPage(   tags$head(crukTheme()),    # Navigation bar (outside central column)   crukNavTitle(     Title = "Cancer Statistics Dashboard",     selectors = 1,     selector1 = selectInput(       "cancer_type",       "Cancer Type:",       choices = c("Breast", "Lung", "Bowel", "Prostate")     )   ),    # Main content (inside central column)   centralColumn(     crukTitle("Incidence Trends"),     p("Explore cancer incidence trends over time."),     plotOutput("trendPlot", height = "400px"),     crukTitle("Data Table"),     tableOutput("dataTable"),     p(       "Data source: Cancer Research UK",       class = "text-muted"     )   ) )  server <- function(input, output, session) {   output$trendPlot <- renderPlot({     # Your plotting code   })    output$dataTable <- renderTable({     # Your table code   }) }  shinyApp(ui, server)  # Nested layout with multiple sections ui <- fluidPage(   tags$head(crukTheme()),   crukNavTitle(Title = "Multi-section Dashboard"),   centralColumn(     # Section 1     crukTitle("Overview"),     fluidRow(       column(6, valueBoxOutput("metric1")),       column(6, valueBoxOutput("metric2"))     ),      # Section 2     crukTitle("Detailed Analysis"),     tabsetPanel(       tabPanel("Chart", plotOutput("chart")),       tabPanel("Table", tableOutput("table")),       tabPanel("Summary", verbatimTextOutput("summary"))     ),      # Footer     hr(),     p("Last updated: 2024-10-20", style = "color: #666;")   ) ) } # }

what: Create a CRUK-branded action button — crukButton
title: Create a CRUK-branded action button — crukButton
text: function creates Cancer Research UK branded action button Shiny applications. wraps shiny::actionButton() CRUK-specific styling optional Google Material Symbols icons.

title: Create a CRUK-branded action button — crukButton
code: crukButton(inputId, text, type = "primary", icon = NULL, ...)

title: Create a CRUK-branded action button — crukButton
text: inputId input slot used access value. text button label text display. type Character string specifying button style. Either "primary" (default, magenta background) "secondary" (white background magenta border). icon Optional character string specifying Google Material Symbol display. Must one : "upload_file", "download", "check_circle", "open_in_new", "content_copy", "mail", "share". Default NULL (icon). ... Additional arguments passed shiny::actionButton().

title: Create a CRUK-branded action button — crukButton
text: Shiny action button tag CRUK branding attached CSS/icon   dependencies.

title: Create a CRUK-branded action button — crukButton
code: if (interactive()) {   library(shiny)    ui <- fluidPage(     crukButton("btn1", "Primary Button", type = "primary"),     crukButton("btn2", "Download", type = "secondary", icon = "download"),     crukButton("btn3", "Share", icon = "share")   )    server <- function(input, output, session) {     observeEvent(input$btn1, {       print("Button clicked!")     })   }    shinyApp(ui, server) }

title: CRUK HTML dependency — crukCSSDependency
text: Attaches CSS resources shinyCRUK components

title: CRUK HTML dependency — crukCSSDependency
code: crukCSSDependency()

title: CRUK HTML dependency — crukCSSDependency
text: htmlDependency object

what: CRUK Footer with Legal Information and Branding — crukFooter
title: CRUK Footer with Legal Information and Branding — crukFooter
text: Creates comprehensive footer section CRUK applications containing CRUK tagline, legal links, regulatory logos, optionally citation contact information. footer provides users terms conditions, privacy policy, regulatory information styled, brand-compliant format.

title: CRUK Footer with Legal Information and Branding — crukFooter
code: crukFooter(includeContact = TRUE)

title: CRUK Footer with Legal Information and Branding — crukFooter
text: includeContact Logical. TRUE (default), includes info-footer section containing CRUK logo, data citation guidelines, contact information. Set FALSE omit section show tagline, legal links, regulatory information. useful apps contact/citation information relevant.

title: CRUK Footer with Legal Information and Branding — crukFooter
text: htmltools::tagList containing: CSS dependency footer styling (crukFooter.css) (Optional) Info section CRUK logo, citation guidelines,       contact details CRUK tagline: "Together beating cancer" Legal links: Terms conditions, Privacy, Modern slavery statement,       Cookies Fundraising Regulator logo Registered charity company information

title: CRUK Footer with Legal Information and Branding — crukFooter
text: function automatically detects whether used Shiny app static HTML context (e.g., Quarto) handles logo resources appropriately.

title: CRUK Footer with Legal Information and Branding — crukFooter
text: Optional Info Section (includeContact = TRUE): CRUK Logo (linked main website), Citation Guidelines (instructions reusing content), Contact Information (cancerintelligence@cancer.org.uk, 10 working days response time). Always Included: Tagline ("Together beating cancer"), Legal Links (Terms conditions, Privacy statement, Modern slavery statement, Cookies policy), Regulatory Information (Fundraising Regulator logo, charity registration numbers, company details, registered address: 2 Redman Place, London, E20 1JQ).

title: CRUK Footer with Legal Information and Branding — crukFooter
text: footer styled responsively via crukFooter.css: Citation contact information displayed two-column layout desktop, stacking mobile (includeContact = TRUE). Legal links presented horizontal list desktop, wrapping mobile. logos text scale appropriately different screen sizes.

title: CRUK Footer with Legal Information and Branding — crukFooter
text: function automatically detects rendering context: Shiny Apps Uses shiny::addResourcePath() serve logo images Static HTML (Quarto/RMarkdown) Uses knitr::image_uri()     embed logos base64-encoded data URIs ensures footer works correctly interactive static documents.

title: CRUK Footer with Legal Information and Branding — crukFooter
text: External HTML files (includeContact = TRUE):     inst/markdown/citation-text.html     inst/markdown/email-site-text.html CSS: inst/www/css/crukFooter.css Logos: inst/www/images/cruk-logo.svg     inst/www/images/logo-fundraising-regulator-reg.svg Required packages: bslib (layout_column_wrap)

title: CRUK Footer with Legal Information and Branding — crukFooter
text: footer placed end UI definition, typically last element within centralColumn bottom page outside central column.

title: CRUK Footer with Legal Information and Branding — crukFooter
code:
# Full footer with contact information (default) crukFooter() #>
#>

#>
#>
#> #> #>

You are welcome to reuse this Cancer Research UK-produced content for #> your own work. Please remember to acknowledge the original data sources #> for each chart and ensure you retain the CRUK logo in the image. Please #> note that due to differences in data collection, comparison of #> performance should not be made between nations.

#> #> #>

If you have any questions regarding the data or the interpretation of #> this data, or if you are having any issues with the website please #> contact:
#> cancerintelligence@cancer.org.uk (please allow #> up to 10 working days for a response)

#>

See our complete statistics pages on #> cancerresearch.org

#>
#>
#>
#>

#> Together we are #>
#> beating cancer #>

#>

#>
    #>
  • #> Terms and conditions #>
    • #>
  • #> Privacy #>
    • #>

    • #>

Registered company in England and Wales (4325234) and the Isle of Man (5713F). Registered address: 2 Redman Place, London, E20 1JQ.

Registered company in England and Wales (4325234) and the Isle of Man (5713F). Registered address: 2 Redman Place, London, E20 1JQ.
# In an interactive Shiny session if (interactive()) { crukFooter() } if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Complete Shiny app with full footer library(shiny) library(shinyCRUK) ui <- fluidPage( tags$head(crukTheme()), crukNavTitle(Title = "Cancer Statistics Dashboard"), centralColumn( crukTitle("Welcome"), p("This dashboard presents cancer incidence and survival statistics."), plotOutput("mainPlot"), # Full footer with contact info crukFooter() ) ) server <- function(input, output, session) { output$mainPlot <- renderPlot({ plot(1:10) }) } shinyApp(ui, server) # App without contact information (e.g., internal tool) ui <- fluidPage( tags$head(crukTheme()), crukNavTitle(Title = "Internal Data Tool"), centralColumn( crukTitle("Analysis"), p("Internal use only - no public contact needed") ), # Footer without contact section crukFooter(includeContact = FALSE) ) # In a Quarto document (full footer) crukFooter() # In a Quarto document (minimal footer) crukFooter(includeContact = FALSE) } # }

title: CRUK Google analytics and cookie banner — crukGA
code: crukGA()

title: CRUK Google analytics and cookie banner — crukGA
text: Adds google analytics shiny dashboard along necessary cookie banner

title: CRUK Google analytics and cookie banner — crukGA
code: htmltools::tags$head(   crukGA() ) #>

title: CRUK Stacked Logo — crukLogo
text: Returns standard Cancer Research UK stacked logo SVG image element. primary CRUK logo format, featuring logo mark text. logo assigned CSS class `cruk-logo` styling purposes.

title: CRUK Stacked Logo — crukLogo
code: crukLogo(height = "50px", width = "auto", class = "")

title: CRUK Stacked Logo — crukLogo
text: height Character string specifying logo height. Must valid CSS width Character string specifying logo width. Must valid CSS unit. Default "auto" maintain aspect ratio. Note: Specifying height width may distort logo aspect ratio match original. class Character string character vector additional CSS class names apply logo element. providing multiple classes, use character vector: `c("class1", "class2")`. added alongside default `"cruk-logo"` class. Default empty string.

title: CRUK Stacked Logo — crukLogo
text: `htmltools::tags$img` element containing CRUK stacked logo   SVG specified dimensions CSS classes.

title: CRUK Stacked Logo — crukLogo
text: Format: SVG (Scalable Vector Graphics)   Orientation: Cancer Research UK 3 lines   Default class: `"cruk-logo"`   File location: `inst/www/images/cruk-logo.svg`

title: CRUK Stacked Logo — crukLogo
text: preferred CRUK logo format use cases. Use   [crukLogoWide()] vertical space limited   stacked format fit layout requirements.

title: CRUK Stacked Logo — crukLogo
code: # Basic usage with default size (50px height) crukLogo() #> # Custom height crukLogo(height = "75px") #> # Custom height and width (use carefully to avoid distortion) crukLogo(height = "60px", width = "60px") #> # Add custom CSS classes crukLogo(class = "my-custom-class") #> # Multiple custom classes crukLogo(height = "100px", class = c("logo-header", "fade-in")) #> # Using CSS units other than pixels crukLogo(height = "5rem") #> crukLogo(height = "10vh") #>

title: CRUK Wide Logo — crukLogoWide
text: Returns wide (horizontal) version Cancer Research UK logo PNG image element. logo assigned CSS class `"cruk-logo-wide"` styling purposes.

title: CRUK Wide Logo — crukLogoWide
code: crukLogoWide(height = "50px", width = "auto", class = "")

title: CRUK Wide Logo — crukLogoWide
text: height Character string specifying logo height. Must valid CSS unit (e.g., "50px", "3rem", "10%"). Default "50px". width Character string specifying logo width. Must valid CSS unit. Default "auto" maintain aspect ratio. Note: Specifying height width may distort logo aspect ratio match original. class Character string character vector additional CSS class names apply logo element. providing multiple classes, use character vector: `c("class1", "class2")`. added alongside default `"cruk-logo-wide"` class. Default empty string.

title: CRUK Wide Logo — crukLogoWide
text: `htmltools::tags$img` element containing CRUK wide logo   PNG specified dimensions CSS classes.

title: CRUK Wide Logo — crukLogoWide
text: Format: PNG (Portable Network Graphics)   Orientation: Wide (logo beside text)   Default class: `"cruk-logo-wide"`   File location: `inst/www/images/cruk-logo-wide.png`

title: CRUK Wide Logo — crukLogoWide
text: Use wide logo format **sparingly** : Horizontal space constrained vertical space available   stacked logo ([crukLogo()]) fit layout   Designing wide/landscape orientations (e.g., desktop navigation bars) stacked logo ([crukLogo()]) preferred format   use cases per CRUK brand guidelines.

title: CRUK Wide Logo — crukLogoWide
code: # Basic usage with default size (50px height) crukLogoWide() #> # Custom height for navigation bar crukLogoWide(height = "35px") #> # Custom height and width crukLogoWide(height = "40px", width = "200px") #> # Add custom CSS classes crukLogoWide(class = "navbar-logo") #> # Multiple custom classes crukLogoWide(height = "45px", class = c("logo-desktop", "float-right")) #>

title: CRUK-branded Navigation Title — crukNavTitle
text: Themes navigation bar CRUK logo. Accomodates mobile layout two selectors

title: CRUK-branded Navigation Title — crukNavTitle
code: crukNavTitle(Title = "", selectors = 0, selector1 = NULL, selector2 = NULL)

title: CRUK-branded Navigation Title — crukNavTitle
text: Title Character string. title text display navigation bar. Can include HTML markup. Default empty string. selectors Integer (0, 1, 2). number selector elements include navigation bar. determines responsive layout behavior: 0: Simple layout title stacked logo 1: Layout title, one selector, responsive logo switching 2: Layout title, two selectors, responsive logo switching Default 0. selector1 Shiny UI element NULL. first selector display navigation bar. used selectors >= 1. Typically selectInput(), pickerInput(), similar input element. Default NULL. selector2 Shiny UI element NULL. second selector display navigation bar. used selectors == 2. Typically selectInput(), pickerInput(), similar input element. Default NULL.

title: CRUK-branded Navigation Title — crukNavTitle
text: htmltools::tagList containing styled navigation bar   embedded CSS responsive behavior.

title: CRUK-branded Navigation Title — crukNavTitle
text: function creates different layouts based screen width: Mobile (< 992px) Vertical layout stacked logo (50px height),     full-width selectors stacked title Desktop (>= 992px) Horizontal layout wide logo (35px 70px height),     selectors positioned alongside title (400px width )

title: CRUK-branded Navigation Title — crukNavTitle
text: title wrapped actionLink id "app_title_link", allowing observe clicks observeEvent(input$app_title_link, ...).

title: CRUK-branded Navigation Title — crukNavTitle
text: Requires shinyCRUK package logo assets inst/www/images/: cruk-logo.svg - Stacked logo mobile view cruk-logo-wide.png - Wide logo desktop view

title: CRUK-branded Navigation Title — crukNavTitle
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Simple navigation with no selectors crukNavTitle(Title = "Local Stats Data Hub")  # Navigation with one selector crukNavTitle(   Title = "Data Explorer",   selectors = 1,   selector1 = selectInput("dataset", "Choose dataset:", choices = c("A", "B")) )  # Navigation with two selectors crukNavTitle(   Title = "Analysis Dashboard",   selectors = 2,   selector1 = selectInput("year", "Year:", choices = 2020:2024),   selector2 = selectInput("cancer-site", "Cancer site:",     choices = c("All cancers", "Breast", "Bowel", "Lung", "Prostate")   ) ) } # }

what: Round Numbers Following Cancer Intelligence Guidelines — crukRounding
title: Round Numbers Following Cancer Intelligence Guidelines — crukRounding
text: Formats numbers according Cancer Intelligence rounding rules, providing human-readable descriptions appropriate precision levels based magnitude. Returns descriptive text like "around 1,200" "5,000".

title: Round Numbers Following Cancer Intelligence Guidelines — crukRounding
code: crukRounding(number, case = "lower")

title: Round Numbers Following Cancer Intelligence Guidelines — crukRounding
text: number Numeric vector values format. Can integers decimals. case Character string specifying text case. Either "lower" (default) lowercase "upper" sentence case output.

title: Round Numbers Following Cancer Intelligence Guidelines — crukRounding
text: Character vector formatted number descriptions thousand   separators descriptive qualifiers (around, nearly, , less ).

title: Round Numbers Following Cancer Intelligence Guidelines — crukRounding
text: function applies different rounding rules based number magnitude: Numbers >= 100,000: Rounded 3 significant figures qualifiers Numbers >= 1,000: Rounded nearest 100 "around" prefix Numbers >= 100: Rounded nearest 10 "around" prefix Numbers >= 10: Rounded nearest 5 "around" prefix Numbers < 10: Simplified "around 10", "around 5", "less 5" Special handling applied avoid ties 0.5 boundaries adding small increments rounding.

title: Round Numbers Following Cancer Intelligence Guidelines — crukRounding
code: crukRounding(1200) #> [1] "around 1,200" # Returns: "around 1,200"  crukRounding(1200, case = "upper") #> [1] "Around 1,200" # Returns: "Around 1,200"  crukRounding(156789) #> [1] "nearly 157,000" # Returns: "around 157,000"  crukRounding(c(3, 25, 500, 5000, 50000)) #> [1] "less than 5" # Returns vectorized output for multiple values  crukRounding(999999) #> [1] "around 1,000,000" # Returns: "around 1,000,000"

what: Round and Format Percentages with Descriptive Text — crukRoundingPercentage
title: Round and Format Percentages with Descriptive Text — crukRoundingPercentage
text: Converts numeric proportions (0-1) human-readable text descriptions following CRUK style guidelines. Provides intuitive phrases like "1 4" alongside percentage values. value integer divide 100 first.

title: Round and Format Percentages with Descriptive Text — crukRoundingPercentage
code: crukRoundingPercentage(number, case = "lower", digits = 3)

title: Round and Format Percentages with Descriptive Text — crukRoundingPercentage
text: number Numeric vector proportions 0 1. case Character string specifying text case. Either "lower" (default) lowercase "upper" sentence case output. digits Integer specifying number decimal places percentage display. Default 3.

title: Round and Format Percentages with Descriptive Text — crukRoundingPercentage
text: Character vector formatted percentage descriptions.

title: Round and Format Percentages with Descriptive Text — crukRoundingPercentage
code: crukRoundingPercentage(0.25) #> [1] "1 in 4 (25%)" # Returns: "1 in 4 (25%)"  crukRoundingPercentage(0.33, digits = 2) #> [1] "1 in 3 (33%)" # Returns: "1 in 3 (33%)"  crukRoundingPercentage(0.4567, case = "lower") #> [1] "almost 1 in 2 (45.7%)" # Returns: "almost 1 in 2 (45.7%)"  crukRoundingPercentage(0.4567, case = "upper") #> [1] "Almost 1 in 2 (45.7%)" # Returns: "Almost 1 in 2 (45.7%)"

what: Apply CRUK Brand Theme to Shiny Application — crukTheme
title: Apply CRUK Brand Theme to Shiny Application — crukTheme
text: Adds Cancer Research UK branding styling Shiny application loading CRUK CSS theme file. function called within UI definition, typically inside tags$head(), apply consistent CRUK styling across entire application.

title: Apply CRUK Brand Theme to Shiny Application — crukTheme
code: crukTheme()

title: Apply CRUK Brand Theme to Shiny Application — crukTheme

what: Page Title with Optional Subheading — crukTitle
title: Page Title with Optional Subheading — crukTitle
text: Creates styled page title element optional subheading decorative horizontal rule. function generates main heading (h1) content pages CRUK applications, providing consistent typography spacing per CRUK brand guidelines.

title: Page Title with Optional Subheading — crukTitle
code: crukTitle(Title, Subheading = "")

title: Page Title with Optional Subheading — crukTitle
text: Title Character string. main page title text display h1 heading. concise, descriptive title page content. Required. Subheading Character string. Optional subheading text display main title. Use provide additional context description page. Appears smaller font title. Default empty string (subheading).

title: Page Title with Optional Subheading — crukTitle
text: htmltools::tagList containing: CSS dependency title styling (crukTitle.css) div container class "cruk-title-container" h1 element main title (Progress Medium font) p element subheading (Poppins font), provided thick horizontal rule (hr) visual separator

title: Page Title with Optional Subheading — crukTitle
text: title appears Progress Medium font, followed optional subheading Poppins font, distinctive thick horizontal rule serves visual separator.

title: Page Title with Optional Subheading — crukTitle
text: title uses CRUK brand typography: Title (h1) Font: Progress Medium, Weight: 500, Margin: 0px Subheading (p) Font: Poppins, Margin: 28px top, 0px sides, Class: "cruk-title-subheading" Horizontal Rule Width: 4rem (~64px), Height: 0.5rem (~8px), Color: Black, Margin: 1rem top, 2rem bottom

title: Page Title with Optional Subheading — crukTitle
text: Place beginning page's content, typically first element within centralColumn. Use descriptive titles clearly communicate page's purpose.

title: Page Title with Optional Subheading — crukTitle
code:
# Simple title without subheading crukTitle("Cancer Incidence") #>
#>

Cancer Incidence

#>
#>
# Title with descriptive subheading crukTitle( "Incidence for Common Cancers", "Latest cancer incidence statistics for the years 2017-2019" ) #>
#>

Incidence for Common Cancers

#>

Latest cancer incidence statistics for the years 2017-2019

#>
#>
# Longer, more descriptive title and subheading crukTitle( "Five-Year Survival Rates by Cancer Type", "Age-standardised net survival for adults diagnosed 2013-2017, followed up to 2018" ) #>
#>

Five-Year Survival Rates by Cancer Type

#>

Age-standardised net survival for adults diagnosed 2013-2017, followed up to 2018

#>
#>
# Title only (empty subheading is default) crukTitle("Data Methods and Sources") #>
#>

Data Methods and Sources

#>
#>
if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # In a complete Shiny app library(shiny) library(shinyCRUK) ui <- fluidPage( tags$head(crukTheme()), crukNavTitle(Title = "Cancer Statistics Dashboard"), centralColumn( # Page title at the start of content crukTitle( "Incidence Trends", "Explore cancer incidence rates from 2000 to 2020" ), # Main page content follows plotOutput("incidencePlot"), tableOutput("incidenceTable") ) ) } # }

title: CRUK-Branded Value Box — crukValueBox
text: Creates branded value box component displaying key metrics statistics CRUK applications. value box features title, prominent value display, optional icon, detail text, styled according CRUK design guidelines.

title: CRUK-Branded Value Box — crukValueBox
code: crukValueBox(   id,   title = "",   value = "",   icon = "",   icon_colour = "#00007e",   detail = "" )

title: CRUK-Branded Value Box — crukValueBox
text: id Character string. HTML id attribute value box element. Used targeting CSS JavaScript. Required. title Character string. title text displayed top value box, describing value represents. horizontal rule appears title. Default empty string. value Character string numeric. main value display prominently value box (e.g., "250", "45.2 Progress Medium font. Default empty string. icon Shiny icon object Material Symbols icon. icon display showcase area left side value box. Can created shiny::icon() Font Awesome icons icon systems. Default empty string (icon). icon_colour Character string. color showcase icon, specified CSS color value (hex code, RGB, named color). Default "#00007e" (CRUK dark blue). Note: Uses British spelling align CRUK conventions. detail Character string. Optional detail text displayed main value, providing additional context information. Appears smaller text. Default empty string.

title: CRUK-Branded Value Box — crukValueBox
text: bslib::value_box object attached CSS dependencies   CRUK styling Google Material Symbols font. value box includes: Title horizontal rule separator Large, prominent value display forward arrow icon Left-aligned showcase area custom icon Detail text value White background black text (CRUK theme)

title: CRUK-Branded Value Box — crukValueBox
text: Value boxes useful highlighting important numbers, KPIs, summary statistics dashboards data visualizations. CRUK value box includes distinctive horizontal rule title forward arrow icon next value.

title: CRUK-Branded Value Box — crukValueBox
text: value box uses custom CSS (crukValueBox.css) applies: Progress Medium font value display CRUK-styled horizontal rule title Material Symbols "arrow_forward" icon next value Showcase layout icon left (25% width) White background black text

title: CRUK-Branded Value Box — crukValueBox
text: function loads: crukValueBox.css - Custom CRUK value box styling Google Material Symbols Outlined font - arrow icon bslib package - base value box component

title: CRUK-Branded Value Box — crukValueBox
text: Use concise titles clearly describe metric Format values appropriately (e.g., add commas thousands, % signs) Choose icons visually represent metric Use default CRUK blue icons unless brand guidelines specify otherwise Keep detail text brief - one two lines maximum

title: CRUK-Branded Value Box — crukValueBox
code:
# Basic value box with title and value crukValueBox( id = "valueBox1", title = "Total Cases", value = "250" ) #>
#>
#>
#>
#>
#> #>
#>
#>

#> Total Cases #>

#>

#>
#> 250 #> arrow_forward #>
#> #>
#>
#>
#> #>
#>
# Value box with icon and detail crukValueBox( id = "valueBox2", title = "Average Weight", value = "74.5 kg", icon = shiny::icon("weight-scale"), detail = "Based on 1,234 patients" ) #>
#>
#>
#>
#>
#> #> #> #>
#>
#>

#> Average Weight #>

#>

#>
#> 74.5 kg #> arrow_forward #>
#> Based on 1,234 patients #>
#>
#>
#> #>
#>
# Value box with custom icon color crukValueBox( id = "valueBox3", title = "Survival Rate", value = "89.2%", icon = shiny::icon("heart-pulse"), icon_colour = "#009cee", detail = "5-year net survival" ) #>
#>
#>
#>
#>
#> #> #> #>
#>
#>

#> Survival Rate #>

#>

#>
#> 89.2% #> arrow_forward #>
#> 5-year net survival #>
#>
#>
#> #>
#>
# Multiple value boxes in a layout if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ library(shiny) library(shinyCRUK) ui <- fluidPage( tags$head(crukTheme()), crukNavTitle(Title = "Cancer Statistics Dashboard"), centralColumn( crukTitle("Key Metrics", "Overview of cancer statistics"), # Layout value boxes in a grid bslib::layout_column_wrap( width = 1 / 3, crukValueBox( id = "incidence", title = "New Cases", value = "367,000", icon = shiny::icon("user-plus"), detail = "Annual incidence in UK" ), crukValueBox( id = "survival", title = "Survival Rate", value = "50%", icon = shiny::icon("heart"), icon_colour = "#e40087", detail = "10-year survival" ), crukValueBox( id = "mortality", title = "Deaths", value = "167,000", icon = shiny::icon("chart-line-down"), detail = "Annual mortality in UK" ) ) ) ) server <- function(input, output, session) { # Server logic here } shinyApp(ui, server) # Reactive value boxes with server-side updates ui <- fluidPage( tags$head(crukTheme()), crukNavTitle(Title = "Live Dashboard"), centralColumn( selectInput("cancer_type", "Cancer Type:", choices = c("Breast", "Lung", "Bowel") ), uiOutput("dynamicValueBoxes") ) ) server <- function(input, output, session) { output$dynamicValueBoxes <- renderUI({ # Get data based on selection cases <- switch(input$cancer_type, "Breast" = "55,900", "Lung" = "48,500", "Bowel" = "42,900" ) bslib::layout_column_wrap( width = 1 / 2, crukValueBox( id = "cases_box", title = paste(input$cancer_type, "Cancer Cases"), value = cases, icon = shiny::icon("hospital"), detail = "Annual incidence" ), crukValueBox( id = "rate_box", title = "Incidence Rate", value = "123.4", icon = shiny::icon("chart-line"), detail = "Per 100,000 population" ) ) }) } shinyApp(ui, server) } # }

what: Last Reviewed Date with Optional Audience Tag — lastReview
title: Last Reviewed Date with Optional Audience Tag — lastReview
text: Displays last review date page content optional audience tag (Health Professionals Internal ). component helps users understand currency information intended audience.

title: Last Reviewed Date with Optional Audience Tag — lastReview
code: lastReview(last_review_date, tag = NULL)  lastReviewHP(last_review_date)  lastReviewInternal(last_review_date)

title: Last Reviewed Date with Optional Audience Tag — lastReview
text: last_review_date Character string Date object. date page content last reviewed. Must format "DD Month YYYY" (e.g., "08 July 2025") Date object automatically converted format. Required. tag Character string NULL. audience tag display. Options : "health professional" "hp" - Blue tag health     professional content "internal" - Pink tag internal-content NULL (default) - tag, shows review date

title: Last Reviewed Date with Optional Audience Tag — lastReview
text: htmltools::div element containing: Optional audience tag (specified) appropriate styling Last reviewed date right-aligned format "DD Month YYYY" CSS dependency styling

title: Last Reviewed Date with Optional Audience Tag — lastReview
text: review date appears right-aligned flex container, optional tag appearing left. component typically placed top page content provide context data freshness access restrictions.

title: Last Reviewed Date with Optional Audience Tag — lastReview
text: function enforces consistent date format: "DD Month YYYY" (e.g., "08 July 2025", "01 January 2024"). can provide: character string already "DD Month YYYY" format UK date string "DD/MM/YYYY" format (e.g., "08/07/2025") Date object (e.g., .Date("2025-07-08"))     automatically formatted ISO date string (e.g., "2025-07-08") parsed     formatted Invalid formats produce error guidance expected format. function take UK date format (DD/MM/YYYY), .e. "08/07/2025" 8th July 2025, 7th August 2025 (US format). may want double check format data frame.

title: Last Reviewed Date with Optional Audience Tag — lastReview
text: Health Professional  Background: Light blue (#e2f4fd) Text: Blue-grey (#52627a) Label: "Health professionals"  Internal  Background: Light pink (#fff0f8) Text: Pink (#cc006c) Label: "Internal "

title: Last Reviewed Date with Optional Audience Tag — lastReview
text: Place component top page content, typically immediately crukTitle first element within centralColumn.

title: Last Reviewed Date with Optional Audience Tag — lastReview
code: # Correct format - character string if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ lastReview("08 July 2025") lastReview("01 January 2024", tag = "internal")  # UK date format (DD/MM/YYYY) lastReview("08/07/2025") # 8th July 2025 lastReview("15/03/2024", tag = "hp") # 15th March 2024 lastReview("1/1/2025", tag = "internal") # 1st January 2025  # Using Date objects (automatically formatted) lastReview(as.Date("2025-07-08")) lastReview(Sys.Date(), tag = "hp")  # Using ISO date strings (automatically formatted) lastReview("2025-07-08", tag = "health professional")  # All of these produce: "Last reviewed: 08 July 2025" lastReview("08 July 2025") lastReview("08/07/2025") lastReview(as.Date("2025-07-08")) lastReview("2025-07-08")  # These will produce errors: lastReview("July 8, 2025") # Wrong format (US style) lastReview("32/01/2025") # Invalid date (no 32nd day) } # }

title: Create
text: Creates styled navigation box provides quick links sections within page, along button appears scrolling allows users return top page.

title: Create
code: onThisPage(..., include_top_button = TRUE)

title: Create
text: ... Pairs values first element div ID link second element display text link. pair provided vector `c(id, text)`. div ID can provided without leading `#`. include_top_button Logical. Whether include scroll--top button appears scrolling 200px. Default `TRUE`.

title: Create
text: `tagList` containing styled navigation box, Material Symbols   dependencies, optionally scroll--top button JavaScript   functionality.

title: Create
text: function creates two main components: fixed navigation box styled links page sections scroll--top button appears user scrolls     200px smoothly returns top clicked function automatically includes: CSS styling `css/onThisPage.css` Google Material Symbols Sharp font icons JavaScript scroll detection smooth scrolling behavior

title: Create
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # In your Shiny UI ui <- fluidPage(   onThisPage(     c("introduction", "Introduction"),     c("methods", "Methods"),     c("results", "Results"),     c("conclusion", "Conclusion")   ),    # Your page content with matching IDs   div(id = "introduction", h2("Introduction"), p("...")),   div(id = "methods", h2("Methods"), p("...")),   div(id = "results", h2("Results"), p("...")),   div(id = "conclusion", h2("Conclusion"), p("...")) )  # Without the scroll-to-top button onThisPage(   c("section1", "First Section"),   c("section2", "Second Section"),   include_top_button = FALSE ) } # }
